subject: Human Resources
title: Results-Only Work Environments
description: A quick win: understand Results-Only Work Environments.

hook.text: An employee arrives at noon, leaves at three, and outperforms everyone. Their manager wants to discipline them.

buildup.text: ROWE—Results-Only Work Environment—eliminates schedules and location requirements entirely. The only thing that matters is output.

discovery.text: Best Buy's ROWE experiment increased productivity 35% and reduced turnover 90% in departments that adopted it.

twist.text: Best Buy's new CEO killed ROWE in 2013—preferring visible attendance. The battle between trust and control continues.

climax.text: ROWE requires clear goals, measurable outcomes, and managers who evaluate work, not hours—a shift most aren't ready for.

punchline.text: If you trust people to deliver results, stop caring when they do it.

quiz.question: What results did Best Buy's ROWE experiment show?
